note: Accepted PRODUCTION (non-test/fixture) sharded≡exact RESOLVED-edge divergences on this repo. functionsOnly{Exact,Sharded} MUST be 0 (hard failure). A run EXCEEDING this number fails graph-equivalence-check (a resolver regression); a decrease passes with a tighten hint (ratchet). CHARACTERIZATION after the graph-resolution-correctness Phase-0/1 work (Task-0.4 diagnosis): the 128 ≈ ~33 'exact=[] sharded=[hash]' (sharded-only) + 81 'exact=[hash] sharded=[]' (exact-only) + ~14 both-resolve-differently. NOT a single direction — neither engine is the oracle. The sharded-only are a MIX of sharded-correct (exact wrongly DECLINES relative/intra-package dist-routed imports, e.g. logger.info -> core/lib/logger.ts) plus a small residual of param-callback/test-file name-guesses (Phase 3 declines those). The 81 exact-only are re-export chains the export index does not yet follow (Phase 2 closes them in BOTH engines). The 14 both are cross-package same-name mis-narrowings (Phase 3). Progress: Phase 0.1 removed 33 bare-name phantoms (204->171, 261f72b6); Phase 1 removed the sharded JSX double-emissions (one logical JSX component edge recorded twice at adjacent columns -- the tag-name identifier was admitted as a value-reference alongside the JSX-element node; 171->128) with zero collateral (exact-only=81 and both=14 unchanged). SCC divergence is now 0 (was 3). POST-FORMATTING (Prettier baseline): bumped 128 -> 129 — reformatting shifts source columns, surfacing exactly one additional column-misaligned DUPLICATE edge; functionsOnly stays 0 and SCC stays 0, so this is benign column drift from formatting, not a resolver regression. Re-tighten on the next graph-resolution phase.
